description_long: This object is a vintage, industrial-style car with a heavily weathered and rusted exterior, featuring a cylindrical engine compartment and exposed mechanical components. The car has four large, spoked metal wheels, with the rear wheels being significantly larger than the front ones, suggesting it is an early steam or combustion-powered vehicle. The body is primarily constructed from dark, corroded metal, with visible bolts, pipes, and a prominent flywheel on one side. There is no enclosed cabin or roof, and the seating area appears minimal or absent, emphasizing its utilitarian, machine-like design. The overall form is elongated, with a narrow chassis and a pronounced front section that houses the engine. The car's appearance is reminiscent of early 20th-century agricultural or industrial vehicles, possibly a steam tractor or a primitive automobile.
description: A heavily rusted, vintage industrial car with large spoked wheels, exposed engine parts, and a utilitarian, machine-like design. The body is mostly dark metal with visible corrosion and mechanical details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow chassis, cylindrical engine compartment, and the alignment of the four wheels, with the large rear wheels dominating the silhouette.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated body, large spoked rear wheels, smaller front wheels, and exposed flywheel and engine components.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the wide stance of the rear wheels and the back of the engine compartment, with minimal bodywork.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the mechanical details, large rear wheels, and the overall industrial aesthetic.
